report_markdown:
# Your Week: April 7–11, 2026

14 meeting hours. 6 back-to-back blocks. A longest focus window of 1.5 hours across the entire week. You completed 2 of 11 tasks — an 18% completion rate — and had exactly one free evening.

## The Front-Load Collapse

Monday hit a load score of 95, Wednesday 90. Both days logged 5 and 4 meetings respectively, both included boundary violations — an 8am start Monday, a 7pm meeting Wednesday — and both ended with zero tasks completed. Tuesday followed Monday with zero completions despite a lighter 3-meeting day. That's not spillover. That's a system that burned through its reserves in the first 48 hours and spent the rest of the week in recovery mode.

## The Avoidance Loop

Personal tasks were moved 4 times against only 3 total tasks — the move count exceeds the task count. Financial tasks: 0 completed, moved 3 times. These two categories account for 5 tasks and zero completions. Meanwhile, 94 emails sit unread and 11 threads remain unanswered. This isn't a time problem. Things that require you to confront your own life — money, relationships, personal obligations — are being systematically deferred while work tasks at least occasionally get done.

## Boundary Erosion

4 late-night emails sent. 4 of 5 evenings consumed by work. The day started before hours on Monday and ended after hours on Wednesday, with 6 back-to-back blocks compressing whatever daylight remained. When work expands into mornings, evenings, and every gap between meetings, there's no time that belongs to you — which explains why personal tasks keep getting moved. They have nowhere to land.

## The Spending Signal

$110 on food delivery against $45 on dining out. You're eating alone, at a screen, between or after meetings. Fitness spend hit $49.99 — the membership exists, the intention exists — but with one free evening and zero personal task completions, it's unlikely the usage matched the cost. Total spend reached 58.3% of monthly budget in a single week.

**The one honest thing:** Thursday and Friday had load scores of 38 and 35 — you had space, and still completed nothing personal or financial. This isn't about the meetings.

---
*The root cause is that you've allowed your calendar to become the only thing that has a claim on your time, which means anything requiring your own initiative — personal, financial, relational — has no protected space and keeps getting abandoned.*
root_cause: You've allowed your calendar to become the only thing that has a claim on your time, which means anything requiring your own initiative — personal, financial, relational — has no protected space and keeps getting abandoned.
